h2h_summary: Morocco lead the head-to-head with 1 win and 1 draw in 2 meetings, most notably thrashing Scotland 3-0 in the 1998 World Cup group stage.

notable_results: 0-3 Morocco win - World Cup Group Stage - 1998

team_style_home: Scotland play a pragmatic, defensively organized 4-3-3 or 3-4-3 under Steve Clarke, relying on hard work, set-piece threat, and the creativity of McTominay and McGinn to create chances in transition.
team_style_away: Morocco deploy a disciplined 4-2-3-1 that defends from a compact mid-block, then transitions quickly through Hakimi's overlapping runs and Brahim Diaz's creativity in the final third.
wc_history_home: Scotland have appeared at eight previous World Cups (1954-1998) but never progressed beyond the group stage, famously going out on goal difference three times including a memorable 3-2 win over the Netherlands in 1978 featuring Archie Gemmill's iconic goal.
wc_history_away: Morocco became the first African and Arab nation to reach a World Cup semi-final at Qatar 2022, defeating Spain, Portugal, and Belgium en route before losing to eventual champions France.
group_context: Group C features four-time champions Brazil as heavy favourites alongside AFCON champions Morocco, a resurgent Scotland returning to the World Cup for the first time since 1998, and Haiti; Scotland must finish in the top two to advance and face Morocco in a pivotal second-round fixture that could decide who accompanies Brazil into the knockout stage.
strengths_home: Tournament pedigree in attack: McTominay (Serie A Player of the Year) and Adams in excellent pre-tournament form
Set-piece danger: strong aerial threats and rehearsed routines from corners and free-kicks
Tactical discipline and hard-to-beat shape under Steve Clarke's experienced management
strengths_away: Achraf Hakimi as a world-class attacking threat and defensive anchor at right back
Proven big-game mentality from 2022 World Cup semi-final run
Strong collective defensive structure with Amrabat shielding a compact backline and Bounou's elite penalty-saving record
